summary: Session 2: shipped the Copilot CLI transport layer (ai_router/cli_transport.py -- Transport interface, CopilotCliTransport invocation state machine against an injected spawner, TransportResult) and the seat-local catalog lockfile (ai_router/copilot_catalog.py -- dataclasses, restricted-TOML reader/writer, validate_catalog's four fail-closed rules, discover_catalog + --refresh CLI). Wired transport.profile/transports.copilot-cli into router-config.yaml and config.py (api profile provably unchanged); mechanically extracted providers.py's inline dispatch dict. Routed test-generation produced a 38+4-test Layer-1 fake-spawner suite (adapted: fixed 3 mismatched exception-message expectations and 1 real shared-mutable-fixture cross-test bug). Routed code-review (auto-verified) found and fixed 7 issues (timeout-deadline anchoring, a spawn-timeout process leak, a hardcoded post-EOF wait, a missing reap, an overly-narrow except clause, the copilot-cli profile still requiring provider API keys, dead code). Cross-provider session verification ran 5 rounds, each finding one genuine, distinct, reproducible defect (local-overrides merge ordering; three progressively narrower field-shape gaps in the success-path JSON parser converging from content to outputTokens to nested usage.premiumRequests) -- all fixed with regression tests, round 5 VERIFIED clean under an explicit merge-reviewer materiality bar. Final suite: 2432 passed, 5 skipped, one pre-existing unrelated drift_guard failure (two session sets simultaneously in-progress per this set's own deliberate Sequencing-against-Set-077 design) that predates this session throughout. No route() integration yet (Session 3's scope, by design) and no version bump/release (Session 5's scope).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (task_type=analysis, gemini-pro) recommended continuing with claude-code/anthropic/claude-sonnet-5 for Session 3: integration-heavy work extending route()'s existing control flow that the incumbent orchestrator already has fresh context on, not a net-new implementation where a different provider's strengths would argue for a switch.
